articles.content: When clouds parted in early June 2026, satellites glimpsed hints of summers approach in the Bering Sea off Alaskas coast. Sea ice, broken into small fragments, took a few final spins on its way to me… [+2756 chars]

articles.title: No spacecraft has ever landed in the outer solar system — except one: the Huygens probe, which parachuted through Titan's orange haze in 2005 and touched down more than a billion kilometres from Earth in cold that dropped below minus 170 degrees Cels - Space Daily
articles.description: Two decades on, the European Huygens probe is still the only spacecraft ever to land in the outer solar system, after parachuting onto Saturn's moon Titan in 2005.
articles.content: For all the spacecraft humanity has sent into the outer solar system, only one has ever landed there. Everything else, from the Pioneers and Voyagers to Galileo, Cassini and Juno, has flown past or o… [+5562 chars]

articles.author: Ashley Strickland, CNN
articles.title: An unusual strategy helps some tropical butterflies live 25 times longer than their relatives - KSL News
articles.description: Since most butterflies live a short time, a few rare exceptions have stumped scientists. Now, some long-lived tropical butterfly species are shedding light on the secrets of longevity.
articles.content: ATLANTA Since most butterflies live a short time, fluttering between colorful flowers for a few weeks before dying, a few rare exceptions have stumped scientists. Now, some long-lived tropical butter… [+7172 chars]
